assets.creator: Atomic Acres project
assets.modifications: Fifty-five deterministic project-generated textures: nineteen original albedo surfaces plus eighteen tangent-normal and eighteen roughness companions. Pass 31 adds plaster, timber, gunmetal and painted-metal maps; the 2026-07-22 weapon pass derives seven distinct restrained carbine, SMG, LMG, scattergun, sniper, pistol and machine-pistol PBR finish triplets from project-authored gunmetal pixels with no external imagery.

assets.creator: Atomic Acres project / Jigglyclaw
assets.modifications: Original ceremonial grenade authored from Blender primitives through the local Blender MCP socket. No commercial model, texture, or extracted game asset is used. Runtime loading preserves the existing gameplay collider, fuse, damage, networking, and explosion behavior.

assets.creator: Atomic Acres project / Jigglyclaw
assets.format: glTF 2.0 binary, 33 meshes, 27 exported materials, 34336 triangles, 6 semantic breakable windows, 3 semantic route landmarks, 2 modeled transit buses, 4 authored large physical cover assets, 2 authored house furnishing sets, 35 embedded project-authored images, 57 texture bindings and 19 exported PBR materials
assets.modifications: Complete original near-future agricultural test-suburb environment authored deterministically in Blender 4.0.2 from project primitives, project-authored surface textures and the authoritative TypeScript arena/house specification. Pass 27 establishes distinct Verdant Array, Civic Transit and Helio Service route identities through overhead irrigation and grow rails, civil-defence signals and flush evacuation cues, photovoltaic/battery/coolant hardware, rooftop habitat sensors, distant agritech energy silhouettes and bounded contact-grime patches. Presentation hydroponic beds were lowered so they no longer imply false collision. Pass 37 removes residual coplanar and under-20-mm surface conflicts across house floors/walls/ramps, road overlays, bus trim, pipe stacks, boundary corners and beacon rings while retaining procedural gameplay authority. No Activision, Call of Duty, Black Ops, downloaded model, copied texture, logo, signage, map geometry, or extracted game asset is used. The GLB is presentation-only; TypeScript collision, raycasts, ramps, windows, networking, drops and bot navigation remain authoritative.

assets.creator: Atomic Acres project / Jigglyclaw
assets.modifications: Original climbable Rustworks oil-rig environment authored from Blender primitives by the repository generator. Pass 54 aligns the authored ramps and decks with collision authority, removes obsolete tanks, pallets, loose crates, barriers and upper-deck utilities, opens the central lanes, constrains the deck surface to the safety rail so the ocean remains visible, and places six mirrored shipping containers on each of the four perimeter sides. The versioned .blend source and generator remain included for deterministic inspection and rebuilding.

assets.creator: Atomic Acres project / Jigglyclaw
assets.modifications: Direct in-engine Quality Graphics capture of the Atomic Acres Civic Transit bus lane with the slightly funny close-up bus and three genuine runtime SWAT operators in clean idle weapon poses. The frame received a restrained contrast, gamma, saturation, and vignette grade. No generated or external image content is present.

assets.creator: Atomic Acres project / Jigglyclaw
assets.description: Original four-syllable formant-synthesized choir sting singing Hal-le-lu-jah for the Sanctified Frag explosion. No sampled or commercial recording is used.

assets.creator: Quaternius (@Quaternius)
assets.modifications: Swat.gltf is preserved as the reference source. Swat_FirstPersonArms.glb is a Blender 5.1.1 derivative retaining the original arm, hand, finger skin weights and skeleton while removing torso geometry at the authored shoulder seam. The derivative is bundled for continued calibration but is not selected by the current public first-person presentation because it failed the visual grip/framing gate.

assets.creator: Quaternius (@Quaternius)
assets.modifications: Rifle, P90, Shotgun and Pistol source FBX files were converted with Blender 5.1.1 to glTF 2.0 binary with Y-up coordinates and authored animations retained. Runtime materials and presentation transforms are adapted in-engine; weapon balance and authoritative hitscan remain original Atomic Acres code.

rejectedCandidates.creator: para
rejectedCandidates.modifications: Rejected after in-engine framing and deformation review. The original FBX and deterministic audit textures remain versioned outside public/ for provenance, but Vite must never copy them into a release.

excludedLegacyAssets.reason: Removed from the structured release because the owner rejected their low-poly art direction; no Kenney runtime asset ships in this build.
